channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The Baltic undersea cable cutting highlights the internet’s underlying vulnerabilities
item.description: The severing of a key subsea telecoms cable running under Europe’s Baltic Sea has caused a diplomatic incident this week, as both country’s politicians called the act “sabotage”—all while Russia, whom many say is the likely instigator, denies responsibility.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Dangerous global botnet fueling residential proxies is being hit in major crackdown
item.description: Lumen and partners have disrupted the operations of the NSOCKS proxy and the underlying ngioweb botnet.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: US gathers allies to talk AI safety, Trump's vow to undo Biden's AI policy overshadows their work
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has vowed to repeal President Joe Biden's signature artificial intelligence policy when he returns to the White House for a second term. What that actually means for the future of AI technology remains to be seen.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Comcast to spin off cable networks, once star performers for the entertainment giant
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Comcast will spin off many of its cable television networks that were once at the heart of the entertainment giant, with people increasingly swapping out their cable TV subscriptions for streaming platforms.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: It's not just free VPNs, these premium services may be bad for your privacy
item.description: New research unveils the risks of paid Android VPNs, from data leaks to weaker encryption and dubious data sharing. Here's all you need to know.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: The Trump tariffs could create a huge headache for streamers
item.description: With Donald Trump set to return to the White House in January, streaming media and consumer electronics companies have to prepare for disruption ahead. Some of Trump’s policies could arguably benefit the industry.
item.dc:creator: Janko Roettgers, Fast Company

item.title: EU industry group has its eye on Microsoft’s cloud business
item.description: Microsoft’s cloud business is under surveillance in Europe: The association of Cloud Infrastructure Services Providers in Europe (CISPE) has set up new monitoring body to ensure the tech giant complies with a legal settlement to keep cloud licensing practices fair in the region.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Siete formas de implementar su propio LLM
item.description: La inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa es la nueva tecnología que más rápido se está desarrollando en la historia. Está transformando el mundo y, según la Encuesta de población en tiempo real a más de 3.000 adultos que trabajan en EE.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: US commission proposes ‘Manhattan Project-like’ initiative for AI
item.description: A US congressional commission has called for a “Manhattan Project-like” initiative to accelerate artificial intelligence (AI) development, urging Congress to grant the executive branch sweeping, multiyear contracting authority to fund advancements in AI, cloud computing, and data centers.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Australia won't force social media users to share their personal details when child ban takes effect
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia's communications minister said Wednesday the government won't force social media users to hand over their personal information to tech companies, as children younger than 16 are set to be banned from the platforms.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Must-do privacy settings on your iPhone in iOS 18.1
item.description: Apple's latest iOS update introduces advanced artificial intelligence capabilities, which may be capturing and analyzing sensitive information.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Azure AI Foundry tools for changes in AI applications
item.description: The way we use artificial intelligence is changing. Chatbots aren’t going away. We’ll continue to use them to deliver basic, natural language, self-service applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nvidia’s rivals are focusing on building AI inference chips. Here’s what to know
item.description: Building the current crop of artificial intelligence chatbots has relied on specialized computer chips pioneered by Nvidia, which dominates market and made itself the poster child of the AI boom.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft unveils imaging APIs for Windows Copilot Runtime
item.description: Microsoft’s Windows Copilot Runtime, which allows developers to integrate AI capabilities into Windows, is being fitted with AI-backed APIs for image processing. It will also gain access to Phi 3.5 Silica, a custom-built generative AI model for Copilot+ PCs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The dark energy pushing our universe apart may not be what it seems, scientists say
item.description: Distant, ancient galaxies are giving scientists more hints that a mysterious force called dark energy may not be what they thought.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Business Internet Security: Everything You Need to Consider
item.description: Cyber-attacks can cost companies millions of dollars in lost revenue, legal fees, and recovery efforts. A security breach can severely tarnish a company’s reputation and customer trust, making comprehensive internet security crucial for your small business.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft pitches AI 'agents' that can perform tasks on their own at Ignite 2024
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella told customers at a conference in Chicago on Tuesday that the company is teaching a new set of artificial intelligence tools how to "act on our behalf across our work and life.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Forget driverless cars. One company wants autonomous helicopters to spray crops and fight fires
item.description: HENNIKER, N.H. (AP) — When Hector Xu was learning to fly a helicopter in college, he recalled having a few “nasty experiences” while trying to navigate at night.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NIST’s quantum standards: The time for upgrades is now
item.description: Quantum computing is farther along than most realize, and it's time to safeguard against cybersecurity threats posed by those who will use quantum technologies. The post NIST’s quantum standards: The time for upgrades is now first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: This tool is made for the laziest parent ever
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses how an innovative Easy-Way kit turns standard strollers electric, simplifying navigation for parents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Energy Department’s ‘ETAC’ cyber threat center goes operational
item.description: The ETAC has quickly scaled from a pilot project to an operational center at the Energy Department for analyzing cyber threats like Volt Typhoon. The post Energy Department’s ‘ETAC’ cyber threat center goes operational first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Nvidia Blackwell chips face serious heating issues
item.description: Nvidia’s next-generation Blackwell data center processors have significant problems with overheating when installed in high-capacity server racks, forcing redesigns of the racks themselves, according to a report by The Information.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 5 things to know about Trump’s FCC pick
item.description: President-elect Trump has tapped Brendan Carr to be the next chair of the Federal Communications Commission (FCC), teeing the agency up for a likely clash with Big Tech and media companies he has accused of “censoring” conservative views.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Company affiliated with Alex Jones seeks to disqualify The Onion's auction bid on Infowars
item.description: A company affiliated with conspiracy theorist Alex Jones asked a federal judge on Monday to disqualify a bid by the satirical news outlet The Onion to buy Jones' Infowars at a bankruptcy auction, alleging fraud and collusion.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge tosses Huckabee lawsuit against Meta over ads suggesting he endorsed marijuana gummies
item.description: WILMINGTON, Del. (AP) — A federal judge in Delaware on Monday dismissed a lawsuit filed by former Arkansas Gov. Mike Huckabee against social media giant Meta over advertisements using his name and image to sell CBD products.
item.dc:creator: RANDALL CHASE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 14 great preprocessors for developers who love to code
item.description: As much as we love them, programming languages can often feel like straitjackets. They’re a complex bundle of syntactic rules, and if we break them—even once—the compiler starts screaming out error messages.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The dirty little secret of open source contributions
item.description: “Nobody cares if you contribute.” That’s what a Postgres friend said to me during lunch at KubeCon when I suggested that hiring Postgres contributors could be a selling point for customers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The worst passwords you could have in 2024 — or any year
item.description: Don't rely on easy-to-remember passwords too much — because all of us tend to think more alike than we'd think.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, NewsNation

item.title: Apple’s AirPods Pro 2 makes hearing tests as easy as a few taps
item.description: Checking your hearing has gotten easier with the new hearing test feature in AirPods Pro 2. You can take reliable hearing tests using your iPhone or iPad.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
